req.title: System-Admin updates training-course
req.statement: An authenticated system-admin shall be able to update a training-course through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers a UI form for system-admin to update a training-course with mandatory field validation and audit logging. I derived: (1) a positive EP test for a valid full update by an authenticated admin, verifying 200/201 and audit record; (2) a NEG test for missing mandatory fields expecting 400; (3) a BVA test for a title at exactly the maximum allowed length (255 chars); (4) a NEG test for an unauthenticated request expecting 401; (5) a DT test for a forbidden role (non-admin) expecting 403. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that set up and tear down state.

tests.name: test_admin_updates_training_course_valid_payload
preconditions: A training-course record exists (seeded by fixture)
actions: Fill all mandatory fields with valid data
expected: Audit record is written for the update event
trace: R-WEB-046: Expected outcomes - training-course is updated
R-WEB-046: Expected outcomes - audit record written
R-WEB-046: Expected outcomes - response 200 or 201 with entity id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_updates_training_course_valid_payload(
    admin_page,           # <fixture>: authenticated Playwright Page as system-admin
    seeded_course,        # <fixture>: returns {"id": str, "title": str}
    audit_log_client,     # <fixture>: client to query audit records
    app_base_url,         # <fixture>: base URL string
):
    course_id = seeded_course["id"]
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")

    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", "Updated Safety Training")
    page.fill("[data-testid='description']", "Refreshed content")
    page.fill("[data-testid='duration_hours']", "8")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses/{course_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert str(body.get("id")) == str(course_id)

    expect(page.locator("[data-testid='success-banner']")).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written"

tests.name: test_admin_update_course_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400
preconditions: A training-course record exists (seeded by fixture)
expected: Validation error message displayed for the title field
trace: R-WEB-046: Error pathways - 400 on validation error
R-WEB-046: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_admin_update_course_missing_mandatory_fields_returns_400(
    admin_page,
    seeded_course,
    audit_log_client,
    app_base_url,
):
    course_id = seeded_course["id"]
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")

    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", "")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: "/training-courses" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status == 400, f"Expected 400, got {resp.status}"

    error_locator = page.locator("[data-testid='field-error-title']")
    expect(error_locator).to_be_visible()

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "Audit record must not be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_admin_update_course_title_at_max_boundary_length
preconditions: A training-course record exists (seeded by fixture)
actions: Fill title with exactly 255 characters (max boundary)
trace: R-WEB-046: Mandatory fields must be validated
R-WEB-046: Expected outcomes - training-course is updated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

MAX_TITLE_LEN = 255

def test_admin_update_course_title_at_max_boundary_length(
    admin_page,
    seeded_course,
    audit_log_client,
    app_base_url,
):
    course_id = seeded_course["id"]
    page = admin_page
    max_title = "A" * MAX_TITLE_LEN
    assert len(max_title) == MAX_TITLE_LEN

    page.goto(f"{app_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")
    page.fill("[data-testid='title']", max_title)
    page.fill("[data-testid='duration_hours']", "4")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses/{course_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert len(body.get("title", "")) == MAX_TITLE_LEN

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) >= 1, "Audit record not written for boundary-length title update"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_training_course
preconditions: No active session cookie / bearer token
A training-course record exists (seeded by fixture)
actions: Send PUT/PATCH request to /training-courses/{id} without auth credentials
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_update_training_course(
    seeded_course,
    audit_log_client,
    app_api_base_url,     # <fixture>: API base URL string
):
    course_id = seeded_course["id"]
    payload = {
        "title": "Unauthorized Update Attempt",
        "duration_hours": 2
    }

    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{app_api_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers={},          # deliberately no Authorization header
        timeout=10,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated request"

tests.name: test_non_admin_role_cannot_update_training_course
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role: employee (not system-admin)
A training-course record exists (seeded by fixture)
actions: Send PUT/PATCH request to /training-courses/{id} with employee-role credentials
trace: R-WEB-046: Error pathways - 403 if role forbidden
tests.executable:
import pytest
import requests

def test_non_admin_role_cannot_update_training_course(
    employee_auth_headers,  # <fixture>: dict with Authorization header for employee role
    seeded_course,
    audit_log_client,
    app_api_base_url,
):
    course_id = seeded_course["id"]
    payload = {
        "title": "Employee Role Update Attempt",
        "duration_hours": 3
    }

    resp = requests.patch(
        f"{app_api_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}",
        json=payload,
        headers=employee_auth_headers,
        timeout=10,
    )

    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert len(audit_entries) == 0, "No audit record should be written when role is forbidden"

tests.name: test_course_transitions_from_draft_to_published_via_update
preconditions: A training-course record exists in DRAFT state (seeded by fixture)
expected: Training-course status transitions from DRAFT to PUBLISHED
Audit record written with state-change details
trace: R-WEB-046: Expected outcomes - training-course is updated
R-WEB-046: Expected outcomes - audit record written
tests.executable:
import pytest
from playwright.sync_api import expect

def test_course_transitions_from_draft_to_published_via_update(
    admin_page,
    seeded_draft_course,   # <fixture>: returns course in DRAFT state {"id": str}
    audit_log_client,
    app_base_url,
    app_api_base_url,
):
    import requests
    course_id = seeded_draft_course["id"]
    page = admin_page
    page.goto(f"{app_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}/edit")

    page.select_option("[data-testid='status']", "PUBLISHED")

    with page.expect_response(
        lambda r: f"/training-courses/{course_id}" in r.url
        and r.request.method in ("PUT", "PATCH", "POST")
    ) as resp_info:
        page.click("[data-testid='submit-btn']")

    resp = resp_info.value
    assert resp.status in (200, 201), f"Expected 200/201, got {resp.status}"

    verify = requests.get(
        f"{app_api_base_url}/training-courses/{course_id}",
        headers=<admin_auth_headers>,  # <fixture>
        timeout=10,
    )
    assert verify.json()["status"] == "PUBLISHED"

    audit_entries = audit_log_client.get_entries(entity_id=course_id, action="UPDATE")
    assert any(e.get("new_status") == "PUBLISHED" for e in audit_entries), "Audit must record state change"
